heading: Determinations on basis of facts available
label: 1677e Determinations on basis of facts available

(a) generally, which prior to amendment read as follows: “Except with respect to information the verification of which is waived under , the administering authority shall verify all information relied upon in making a final determination in an investigation. In publishing such a determination, the administering authority shall report the methods and procedures used to verify such information. If the administering authority is unable to verify the accuracy of the information submitted, it shall use the best information available to it as the basis for its determination, which may include the information submitted in support of the petition.”Pub. children.text: necessary information is not available on the record, or

children.label: A
children.text: withholds information that has been requested by the administering authority or the Commission under this subtitle,

children.label: B
children.text: fails to provide such information by the deadlines for submission of the information or in the form and manner requested, subject to subsections (c)(1) and (e) of ,section 1677m of this title

children.label: C
children.text: significantly impedes a proceeding under this subtitle, or

children.label: D
children.text: provides such information but the information cannot be verified as provided in ,section 1677m(i) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: may use an inference that is adverse to the interests of that party in selecting from among the facts otherwise available; and

children.label: B
children.text: is not required to determine, or make any adjustments to, a countervailable subsidy rate or weighted average dumping margin based on any assumptions about information the interested party would have provided if the interested party had complied with the request for information.

children.heading: Potential sources of information for adverse inferences
children.label: 2 Potential sources of information for adverse inferences
B
children.text: a final determination in the investigation under this subtitle,

children.label: C
children.text: any previous review under or determination under , orsection 1675 of this titlesection 1675b of this title

children.label: D
children.text: any other information placed on the record.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), when the administering authority or the Commission relies on secondary information rather than on information obtained in the course of an investigation or review, the administering authority or the Commission, as the case may be, shall, to the extent practicable, corroborate that information from independent sources that are reasonably at their disposal.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: The administrative authority and the Commission shall not be required to corroborate any dumping margin or countervailing duty applied in a separate segment of the same proceeding.

children.heading: Subsidy rates and dumping margins in adverse inference determinations
children.label: d Subsidy rates and dumping margins in adverse inference determinations
i
children.text: use a countervailable subsidy rate applied for the same or similar program in a countervailing duty proceeding involving the same country; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if there is no same or similar program, use a countervailable subsidy rate for a subsidy program from a proceeding that the administering authority considers reasonable to use; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an antidumping duty proceeding, use any dumping margin from any segment of the proceeding under the applicable antidumping order.

children.heading: Discretion to apply highest rate
children.label: 2 Discretion to apply highest rate
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the administering authority may apply any of the countervailable subsidy rates or dumping margins specified under that paragraph, including the highest such rate or margin, based on the evaluation by the administering authority of the situation that resulted in the administering authority using an adverse inference in selecting among the facts otherwise available.

children.heading: No obligation to make certain estimates or address certain claims
children.label: 3 No obligation to make certain estimates or address certain claims
A
children.text: to estimate what the countervailable subsidy rate or dumping margin would have been if the interested party found to have failed to cooperate under subsection (b)(1) had cooperated; or

children.label: B
children.text: to demonstrate that the countervailable subsidy rate or dumping margin used by the administering authority reflects an alleged commercial reality of the interested party.
